stages.name: unit_tests
stages.evidence: test/cli_test.zig 'readPathOutsideRootOption rejects out-of-root --input-report and --file (F-5)' asserts an absolute and a `..`-traversal --input-report/--file value is flagged with its option name while the default report path and a relative doc path pass. It failed to compile before the helper existed ('root has no member readPathOutsideRootOption'); full suite green after (519 tests).

stages.name: real_binary_behavior
stages.command: zig build && ./zig-out/bin/zentinel explain m_x --ai-provider stub --input-report <path>; ./zig-out/bin/zentinel --config <path> check
stages.evidence: Before the fix `explain m_secret --input-report /abs/outside.json` exited 0 and surfaced the out-of-root file's mutant (silently accepted). After the fix, on the real binary: absolute and `..`-traversal `--input-report` are rejected with exit 2 and 'error[ZNTL_CLI_INVALID_OPTION]: --input-report must stay within the project root'; absolute and `..` `--config` are rejected with exit 2 and 'error: --config must stay within the project root'; doctest `--mutate --file` and the doctest AI `--file`/positional doc path are guarded the same way; legitimate in-root `--input-report report.json` still works (exit 0).

stages.name: mutation_checks
stages.reason: Task 121 adds a deterministic read-path containment guard at the CLI; the fix is proven by a failing-before/passing-after unit test over the pure containment helper plus a real-binary before/after read proof. Full self-mutation dogfood remains gated to task 085.

residual_risk: Read-side containment reuses the write-side string guard config.isOutsideRoot (rejects absolute paths and `..` segments), so --input-report, --file, the doctest positional doc path, and an explicit --config now honor the same root-containment contract as --output. docs/CLI_SPEC.md is outside task 121's allowed files, so the within-scope resolution is to enforce containment (acceptance criterion's first branch) rather than document an exception; the asymmetry is closed by enforcement.

The new root.zig readPathOutsideRootOption is pure (a string scan over args delegating to config.isOutsideRoot), so it is unit-tested directly; the cli.zig wiring is exercised by the real-binary proof. resolveConfigPath returns error.ConfigOutsideRoot for an out-of-root explicit --config, caught by configPathOrReject into a clean exit-2 message; aiSettings already swallows the config error (AI falls back to defaults rather than reading an out-of-root config).

Containment is string-level (absolute/`..`), matching the audit's confirmed cases; read-side symlink resolution is not added because the read path is an explicit invoker input (no untrusted-checkout attacker), unlike the write side's task-119 symlink guard. Legitimate in-root reads (the default report path, relative configs and doc paths) are unaffected -- the check only fires for an absolute or `..`-escaping value.
